content: Both Self-Refine for LLMs and the Reflexion autonomous agent can be synthesized into a two-tier self-improvement architecture for generalist AI agents, where lightweight Self-Refine handles low-stakes iterative polishing of task outputs and memory-augmented Reflexion handles high-stakes strategic learning from long-horizon task failures. This hierarchical separation solves the common problem of unnecessary computational overhead from full memory-based reflection on every minor output, while retaining the ability to build long-term agent capabilities.
